zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne de Standish於1348年出生在英國藍卡爾斯坦迪什時，她的父親Henry Standish33歲，母親Lady Isolde Lea22歲。她和Sir John de Ashton II至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1405年在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Standish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Standish (Lancashire, now part of Greater Manchester), from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + edisc ‘enclosure, enclosed park’. Standish (Gloucestershire) does not appear to have contributed towards the surname.

History: Myles Standish (c. 1584–1656) was a soldier of fortune, from 1620 captain of the Mayflower Pilgrims at Plymouth Colony. Little is known of his origins and early life, but in his will he claimed to be descended from a leading Catholic family, the Standishes of Standish, Lancashire, England. He also claimed to have been deprived of his inheritance, a claim not confirmed.

variants.name: Standage
meanings: in Leicestershire and Buckinghamshire, the name may alternatively be from Stanedge Grange in Hartington Nether Quarter or Stanage End in Outseats (both Derbyshire); but evidence is needed for this. The placenames derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + ecg ‘edge, escarpment’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne de Standish was born in 1348, in Standish, Lancashire, England, her father, Henry Standish, was 33 and her mother, Lady Isolde Lea, was 22. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Sir John de Ashton II. She died in 1405, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, at the age of 57.

ko.Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, , England: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈턴 운더 리니
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne de Standish(이)가 1348년 영국 랭카실르 스탠디시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Standish(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Isolde Lea(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Ashton II(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1405년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.
